claim: Web MCP reduces token usage and improves agent performance by replacing DOM/screenshot/pixel-math approaches with structured tool calls
actionable: Implement Web MCP on websites instead of relying on screen-scraping agents; define site capabilities as structured tools in declarative or imperative API
source_title: The agent-ready web: Simplify user actions with WebMCP

claim: Improving semantic HTML and accessibility standards makes websites AI-agent-ready by default without additional Web MCP implementation
actionable: Focus on robust accessibility standards, semantic HTML, and page performance as foundation; Web MCP augments rather than replaces these practices
source_title: The agent-ready web: Simplify user actions with WebMCP

claim: Web MCP declarative API automatically generates JSON schema from HTML form attributes (@tool_name, @tool_description, etc)
actionable: Use declarative API for standard HTML forms by adding tool name/description attributes; browser auto-generates schema
source_title: The agent-ready web: Simplify user actions with WebMCP

claim: Web MCP imperative API enables complex multi-step UI flows via custom tool registration with JavaScript and DOM code in execute block
actionable: Use imperative API for complex workflows; register custom tools with schema, write JavaScript functions in execute block, return state to agent
source_title: The agent-ready web: Simplify user actions with WebMCP

claim: Web MCP is currently in early preview stage (Chrome 146+) and API is experimental; implementation details and annotations may change weekly
actionable: Expect breaking changes when adopting Web MCP in production; use Chrome Canary for development and monitor official documentation for API updates
source_title: The agent-ready web: Simplify user actions with WebMCP

claim: Web MCP enables hybrid user control where users can switch between manual browsing and AI agent control on same website
actionable: Design agent-ready websites to allow users to toggle between manual and agent-assisted modes, maintaining UI sync throughout state changes
source_title: The agent-ready web: Simplify user actions with WebMCP

claim: MCP (server-side) and Web MCP (client-side) are complementary but different; Web MCP is specific to browser-based agent interactions
actionable: Use MCP for server-side tool connections; use Web MCP for browser-based agent interactions; they are not interchangeable
source_title: The agent-ready web: Simplify user actions with WebMCP
